message:
feat(ci,build): add browser-profile build coverage to CI and check-ci lane

Add a "Build (browser profile)" step to the GitHub Actions CI workflow
that runs make build-browser, exercising the ASX_PROFILE_BROWSER
compilation path on every push. Wire the step outcome into the
manifest JSON and first-failure detection logic.

Add build-browser to the check-ci prerequisite list in the Makefile
so local CI runs also verify the browser profile builds cleanly.

Update PROFILE_RESOURCE_CLASS_CAPABILITY_MATRIX.md to reflect that
CI now exercises the browser lane (removes that item from the
remaining-work list and updates the open questions section).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -258,6 +258,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-rkql.8","title":"Standalone fuzz harness sanity tests with crash/UB detection logging","description":"## STATUS: INDEPENDENT TRACK — RUN EXISTING CODE UNDER SANITIZERS

## Context
fuzz_differential.c and fuzz_minimize.c already exist and are complete. This bead
runs them under ASan/UBSan to find memory safety issues or undefined behavior.

This bead is on an INDEPENDENT TRACK — it does not depend on the grammar audit
(rkql.1) or C harness audit (rkql.2). It uses the existing code as-is.

## Task
1. Build fuzz_differential with -fsanitize=address,undefined -fno-omit-frame-pointer
2. Run: fuzz_differential --nightly (100K iterations) under ASan+UBSan
3. Build fuzz_minimize with same sanitizer flags
4. Run: fuzz_minimize --selftest under ASan+UBSan
5. Verify determinism under sanitizers: 10K iterations, same seed, run twice,
   compare all digests — must be bit-identical
6. Create tests/fuzz/test_harness_sanity.sh automating all of the above

## JSONL Log Format
{"ts":"...","step":"asan_fuzz_differential","iterations":100000,"status":"pass","findings":0,"duration_ms":...}
{"ts":"...","step":"asan_fuzz_minimize_selftest","status":"pass","findings":0,"duration_ms":...}
{"ts":"...","step":"determinism_check","iterations":10000,"seed":"fixed","status":"pass","digest_match":true}

## Acceptance Criteria
- 100K iterations under ASan+UBSan, zero findings
- All self-tests pass under sanitizers
- Determinism verified under sanitizers (same seed → same digests)
- test_harness_sanity.sh exists with JSONL output
- If sanitizer finds issues, file them as new beads with P0 priority

## Why Independent Track
Sanitizer runs validate the EXISTING code, not the audited/modified code. Running
them early catches lurking UB that might otherwise be masked. Any findings here
inform the audit work (rkql.2) rather than depending on it.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","estimated_minutes":60,"created_at":"2026-03-12T17:30:33.966622688Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T20:11:42.267349564Z","closed_at":"2026-03-12T20:11:42.266429382Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-rkql.8","depends_on_id":"bd-rkql","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T17:30:33.966622688Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

@@ -139,7 +147,8 @@ jobs:
                 {id: "lint-docs", outcome: $lint_docs_outcome, rerun: "make lint-docs"},

                 {id: "lint-anti-butchering", outcome: $lint_anti_butchering_outcome, rerun: "make lint-anti-butchering"},

             }' > "${ASX_CI_ARTIFACT_ROOT}/${lane}.manifest.json"

@@ -283,12 +283,11 @@ The table below maps the crate-root feature/platform restrictions from
 The current C tree still needs these explicit fail-closed controls before it
 can honestly claim equivalence with the upstream feature/platform matrix:
 
-1. CI coverage for the explicit browser profile lane so the documented build surface is exercised continuously.
-2. Compile-time mutual exclusion for browser-specific subprofiles if the C port
+1. Compile-time mutual exclusion for browser-specific subprofiles if the C port
    adopts more than one browser mode.
-3. Explicit compile-time or runtime gating for future native-only module
+2. Explicit compile-time or runtime gating for future native-only module
    families such as TLS, database, messaging, gRPC, and server substrate work.
-4. A single compatibility matrix that states which combinations are supported,
+3. A single compatibility matrix that states which combinations are supported,
    denied, or currently unimplemented rather than leaving absence implicit.
 
 ---
@@ -350,11 +349,11 @@ can honestly claim equivalence with the upstream feature/platform matrix:
 
 ## 12. Open Questions
 
-1. **Browser profile lane:** The runtime, diagnostics, and build surface now
-   expose `ASX_PROFILE_BROWSER`, but CI still does not exercise that lane and
-   the C port still has only one browser mode. We need an explicit decision on
-   whether to formalize multiple browser subprofiles or keep a single browser
-   boundary scaffold until the broader `web`/WASM surface is unblocked.
+1. **Browser profile lane:** The runtime, diagnostics, build surface, and CI
+   check lane now exercise `ASX_PROFILE_BROWSER`, but the C port still has only
+   one browser mode. We need an explicit decision on whether to formalize
+   multiple browser subprofiles or keep a single browser boundary scaffold
+   until the broader `web`/WASM surface is unblocked.
 
 2. **Resource class R0 (micro):** Some IoT targets may need even tighter limits
    than R1 (e.g., 2 regions, 8 tasks). Not currently planned — evaluate when
